rationale: The citing paper’s core framework and hypotheses hinge on the RNP vs INP distinction, and its research gap explicitly states prior work has distinguished “really new” versus “incrementally new” products. The cited paper (“Measuring Preferences for Really New Products”) directly centers on that same distinction and reports key evidence about higher uncertainty for RNPs, making it a plausible substantive/theoretical source rather than an incidental mention. Despite only DOI-level match evidence, this overlap is strong enough to retain for full relation judgment.
